free_text: Production of high-spin isomers in proton induced
Metallic tantalum foils, about 0.1 mm thick.
Proton energy loss due to degrading materials before
the target was always less than 15 MeV.
The foil stack was thin enough, so that activation from
secondary pions and neutrons could be neglected.
Irradiation time was varied from several
minutes to several hours for long-lived isotopes.
Proton current varied from 0.5 to 10 mA in
quadrature with systematic uncertainties due to:
. Yields from less abundant chemical fractions,
. Decay during irradiation and chemical processing,
Entry is received from VM and small
